title: 85 Mathematics 4.   Mark   each   square   of   any   two   columns   by   ‘–’   sign   with   blue   sketch   pen
subject: Mathematics
activityTitle: 85 Mathematics 4.   Mark   each   square   of   any   two   columns   by   ‘–’   sign   with   blue   sketch   pen
materials:
Coloured square paper, adhesive, white sheet,
ruler, pen/pencil.
procedure: Make different squares of two different colours, say red and blue.
observation:
1.   To   find:   2   –   3
Fig.   1
(i)   To subtract 3 from 2, take 2 red squares
(Fig.1). Try to cross 3 red squares from it.
But there are only 2 red squares, so to cross
three, we add one red and one blue as shown
in Fig. 2.
(ii)   Now cross three red squares.   Count the
number and colour of squares left (Fig. 3).
One blue square is left.
So,   2   –   3   =   –1.   [It   is   same   as   doing   2   +   (–3)].
discussion:
This activity is useful in understanding the process of addition of two or
more integers.
92 Laboratory Manual – Elementary Stage
